executive_summary: Problem strongly validated: 16M+ adolescents in LATAM have diagnosed mental disorders with a 70%+ treatment gap, only 3.4 psychiatrists per 100K people, and multiple paid alternatives confirm willingness to pay — score 75/100 (high pain).
score_reasoning:
Score: 75/100
- Complaint Volume: 13/20 (30+ unique threads found across UNICEF, UNDP, PAHO, WHO, Trustpilot, Reddit, academic journals, and news outlets)
- Complaint Recency: 18/20 (88% from last 24 months — UNICEF 2024, UNDP 2024, Frontiers 2024, NBER 2025, OECD 2025, Springer 2025, Trustpilot 2024-2026; 3 undated excluded)
- Pain Intensity Signals: 14/20 (15+ pain markers including 5 quantified costs — $30B annual economic loss, 70% treatment gap, 3.4 psychiatrists/100K, 14K+ on waiting lists in Chile alone, $1,280 user cost on BetterHelp — and 1 borderline WTP signal from freemium conversion data)
- Workaround Evidence: 14/20 (6 distinct workarounds — AI chatbots as pseudo-therapists, free self-help apps, self-diagnosis via search, crisis hotlines for non-crisis needs, US-based expensive platforms, free government/NGO resources with long waits; 2 involve multi-tool stacks)
- Paid Alternatives: 16/20 (7 paid alternatives — BetterHelp/TeenCounseling $65-100/wk, Talkspace $69-109/wk, Terapify ~$20-80/session, Doctoralia $600MXN/session, Calmerry $49-90/wk, Wysa premium, Hola Therapy $115/session; BetterHelp has 7800+ Trustpilot reviews, Talkspace listed on G2/Capterra)
Total: 13 + 18 + 14 + 14 + 16 = 75

data.problem_statement: Adolescents in Latin America face a severe mental health access crisis: 16 million live with diagnosed mental disorders, yet 70%+ never receive treatment due to a critical shortage of specialists (3.4 psychiatrists per 100K vs OECD's 18), budget underinvestment (<3% of health spending), and prohibitive cost of existing telehealth alternatives designed for US/EU markets.

evidence.quote: 15% of children and adolescents aged 10-19 in LAC — around 16 million — live with a diagnosed mental disorder. Over US$30 billion is lost to economies each year. Only 1.8% of public spending in LAC goes to mental health.
More than 70% of people in LAC who need mental health care do not receive it. There are 3.4 psychiatrists per 100,000 people, significantly below the OECD average of over 18.
Teen Counseling rated 2.4/5 on Trustpilot (86 reviews, 47% 1-star). Parent: 'Teencounselling.com has taken almost £700 from me and my child has not received one counselling session.'
